title: Web Automation Sandboxing
concept: Running browsers in isolated virtual displays to protect the host session and local storage data.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of extracting the DOM tree in web automation?

options.text: To isolate browser sessions from each other.
To create a structured view of a webpage for further actions.
To automate form filling in web pages.

exercises.explain: Extracting the DOM tree helps structure the raw HTML, making it easier for the agent to understand and interact with the website.

cards.title: What is Sandboxing?
cards.body: Sandboxing means running programs in a safe, isolated environment. For web browsers, this protects your computer from unwanted changes or data theft.

cards.title: Web Automation Sandboxing
cards.body: Web automation sandboxing refers to running browsers in isolated virtual displays. This means any actions taken within that browser don't affect your main computer or data.

cards.title: Sandbox Analogy
cards.body: Think of sandboxing like building a sand castle. You can shape it however you want with no risk to the real beach around it. Likewise, a browser in a sandbox can browse freely without affecting your computer.

cards.title: How Sandboxing Works
cards.body: When a browser is sandboxed, it operates like a separate entity. If malware tries to access your local files, it won't succeed because it’s trapped inside the sandbox.

cards.title: Myth: Sandboxing Isn't Necessary
cards.body: Some think isolating browsers is overkill. In reality, sandboxing protects your sensitive information and keeps your main system safe from harmful websites.

cards.title: Comparison of Browsing Modes
cards.body: Regular browsing can affect your computer. Sandboxed browsing protects it.

left.text: Accesses the web, may change settings or steal data.

right.text: Isolated, keeps changes within the sandbox.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of web automation sandboxing?

options.text: It protects your main system from threats.
It decreases the amount of data stored.
It stops websites from loading completely.

exercises.explain: The main benefit of sandboxing is to protect your main system from potential threats that could arise from visiting unsafe websites.

exercises.prompt: Sandboxing prevents any changes from affecting the main computer.
exercises.explain: True! Sandboxing creates a safe space for the browser to operate, keeping changes contained.

exercises.prompt: In sandboxing, web browsers run in isolated __________.
exercises.sentence: In sandboxing, web browsers run in isolated virtual displays.
exercises.explain: The blank must be 'virtual displays', which is essential for understanding how sandboxing works.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about sandboxing is true?

options.text: It isolates browser actions from the host system.
It automatically removes ads from the web pages.

exercises.explain: The correct statement is that sandboxing isolates browser actions from the host system, providing a layer of security.

exercises.prompt: What does sandboxing allow web browsers to do safely?

options.text: Access personal files on the main system.
Run tests without affecting the main environment.
Increase the data storage capacity instantly.
Browse the web without an internet connection.

exercises.explain: Sandboxing allows browsers to run tests or access potentially harmful sites without making lasting effects on the main environment, ensuring your system remains secure.
